steps.title: Define variables and constraints
steps.content:
Let $s$ = number of small candles and $l$ = number of large candles. The problem gives us two key constraints:
  • Cost constraint: $4.90s + 11.60l \leq 2200$
  • Quantity constraint: $s + l \geq 200$ (minimum for discounted pricing)
We want to maximize $l$.

steps.title: Minimize small candles to maximize large candles
steps.content:
To get the most large candles for the budget, we need the fewest small candles. The quantity constraint $s + l \geq 200$ creates a minimum threshold. To maximize $l$, we operate at the boundary where $s + l = 200$:
$$s = 200 - l$$
This substitution will express the cost constraint in terms of $l$ alone.

steps.title: Solve for the maximum number of large candles
steps.content:
Substitute $s = 200 - l$ into the cost constraint:
$$4.90(200 - l) + 11.60l \leq 2200$$
Expanding:
$$980 - 4.90l + 11.60l \leq 2200$$
Combining like terms:
$$980 + 6.70l \leq 2200$$
Subtracting 980 from both sides:
$$6.70l \leq 1220$$
Dividing by 6.70:
$$l \leq \frac{1220}{6.70} \approx 182.09$$
Since the owner can only purchase whole candles, the maximum number of large candles is 182.
